description_long: This bungalow features a rustic, weathered wooden exterior with vertical planks and a prominent gable roof covered in overlapping wooden shingles. The roof extends outward to form a large, shaded porch area supported by two simple posts, with a fringe of thatch or straw along the edges for added texture. The main structure is compact and single-storied, with a small, shuttered window visible on the side. The wood appears aged and slightly gray, giving the building a timeworn, rural charm. The porch area is spacious and open, providing shelter and a welcoming entryway. The overall design is utilitarian, with minimal ornamentation and a focus on practical shelter.
description: A rustic, single-story wooden bungalow with a gable shingle roof and a large, shaded porch area supported by posts. The exterior is weathered wood with a small shuttered window and thatched roof accents.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide porch area, the gable roof, and the thatched fringe along the roof edge.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the porch, the single shuttered window, and the sloped roof profile.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the full expanse of the shingled roof and the vertical wooden planks of the main structure.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the porch extension, roof overhang, and the window placement on the side wall.
